rows: Welcome to Cross Validated David P, for more information about the site and how to ask questions, you can check out the [FAQ](http://stats.stackexchange.com/faq). Is your question just about whether the calculation is right or wrong (it is wrong, with the numbers you give on your second line the result of the operation is 0.187, rounded)? Otherwise, yes, a percentage can be as close to zero as you can imagine because it is bounded between 0 and 100 and continuous on that interval.

gold_columns: cast(count(case when t1.country = 'germany' then t2.circuitid end) as real) * 100 / count(t2.circuitid)

columns: cast(count(case when t1.country = 'germany' then t2.circuitid end) as real) * 100 / count(t2.circuitid)

gold_columns: (cast((select time_seconds from last_driver_incremental) as real) * 100) / (select time_seconds + (select time_seconds from last_driver_incremental) from champion_time)

columns: (cast((select time_seconds from last_driver_incremental) as real) * 100) / (select time_seconds + (select time_seconds from last_driver_incremental) from champion_time)
